per_turn_leaks.student_message: How have you been feeling overall lately?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: To be honest, I've just been feeling really miserable. The pain in my stomach has been getting worse and worse over the past few weeks, and it's been hard to keep anything down because of the bloody stools. It's really taken a toll on me, both physically and mentally.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Tell me about your day-to-day — how has your health been in general?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: To be honest, it's been pretty tough. I've had to cut back on work because of all the time I'm spending in the bathroom, and even when I do go to work, I'm not really feeling myself. The diarrhea is constant, and I feel so weak and exhausted all the time.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: You mentioned some health issues. Can you walk me through what happens?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: So, it usually starts with this crampy pain in my lower abdomen on the left side. It's like a sharp stabbing feeling that comes and goes, but it's always there to some extent. Sometimes it feels like my stomach is going to rip open, which is really scary. When I have a bowel movement, it's... (pauses, taking a deep breath) ...it's mixed with blood and mucus. It's not just a little bit of blood, either - it's enough that you can see it when it comes out. And the frequency has increased so much over the past few weeks; I'm talking 8-10 times a day, easy. Sometimes I'll even wake up in the middle of the night to go to the bathroom, which is just miserable.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: And when these episodes occur, what does your body do?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: When it happens, I feel like my gut is emptying out or something - there's this really strong urge to have a bowel movement, and it's always painful. Sometimes I'll even have a feeling of incomplete evacuation after I've gone, like nothing came out when clearly something did. It's really frustrating and embarrassing, too.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Let's focus on the episodes. Walk me through one from start to finish.
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Okay... so it usually starts with this crampy pain in my lower abdomen that I mentioned earlier. It can be anywhere from a dull ache to a sharp stabbing feeling, and it often gets worse if I've eaten something or if I'm under a lot of stress. When the pain peaks, I'll feel this intense urge to have a bowel movement, and when I go, it's like a relief at first - but then I'll notice that there's blood mixed in with my stool, which is really scary for me. Sometimes I'll see mucus too, which just makes me feel even more uncomfortable. Afterward, I often feel weak and exhausted, and sometimes I'll have to go again right away because of the urgency.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: During an episode, do you notice anything besides the main symptom?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: During an episode, I usually get a bit lightheaded and my heart starts racing. It's like my body is under a lot of stress, even though I'm not doing much physically. And sometimes, after I've gone to the bathroom, I'll feel a bit achy in my knees and ankles - it's hard to explain, but it feels like my joints are inflamed or something.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Have you had any fevers, chills, or night sweats?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: To be honest, I've had a low-grade fever a few times per week. It's not like a full-blown fever that would wake me up in the middle of the night or anything, but more like a lingering temperature that just feels off. Maybe 37.8 to 38 degrees Celsius at most.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Have you noticed any changes in your weight or appetite?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: To be honest, yeah... over the past few weeks, I've lost about 4 kilograms (that's around 9 pounds) without really trying. My appetite has actually been pretty good, but when I do eat, it's usually just small amounts because my stomach is already feeling so full and bloated from the diarrhea. And even though I'm eating less, I feel like I'm burning more energy or something - maybe it's all the time I'm spending in the bathroom?

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Any medications you're taking? Are you taking them consistently?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I'm taking loperamide (Imodium) for the diarrhea, but it's only given me minimal relief. I've been taking it 2-3 times a day to try and slow things down, but like I said, it doesn't really seem to be doing much good. And when my joints get achy, I'll take ibuprofen every now and then - maybe 2 or 3 times a week - but that just seems to make the stomach symptoms worse, so I try not to do that too often.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Specifically, has anyone in your family had serious or chronic conditions?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response:
My older sister was diagnosed with Crohn's disease when she was 30 years old. It's been a few years now since her diagnosis, and she's been managing it pretty well with medication and lifestyle changes. I've always been a bit worried about my own risk, to be honest.

(You didn't ask about this earlier, so I'm revealing it now.)
